aah: interjection. Indication of amazement or surprise or enthusiasm.
acid: adjective. Sour, sharp, or biting to the taste; tart; having the taste of vinegar.
aha: interjection. An exclamation of understanding, realization, invention, or recognition.
ain: noun. Alternative form of ayin (“Semitic letter”).
ana: noun. A collection of things associated with a person or place, especially a personal collection of anecdotes or conversations at table
and: conjunction. As a coordinating conjunction; expressing two elements to be taken together or in addition to each other.
ani: noun. Any bird of the genus Crotophaga in the cuckoo family.
aphid: noun. A sap - sucking insect pest of the superfamily Aphidoidea; an aphidian.
cad: noun. A low-bred, presuming person; a mean, vulgar fellow, especially one that cannot be trusted with a lady.
caid: noun. A local governor or leader, especially in North Africa or Moorish Spain; an alcaide.
canid: noun. Any member of the family Canidae, including canines (dogs, wolves, coyotes and jackals) and vulpines (foxes).
cap: noun. A close-fitting hat, either brimless or peaked.
caph: noun. Alternative spelling of kaph (letter of some Semitic alphabets).
chad: noun. Small pieces of paper punched out from the edges of continuous stationery, or from ballot papers, paper tape, punched cards, etc.
chain: noun. A series of interconnected rings or links usually made of metal.
chi: noun. The twenty-second letter of the Classical and Modern Greek alphabets.
chia: noun. A Mexican sage grown for its edible seeds, Salvia hispanica.
chin: noun. The bottom of a face, the typically jutting jawline below the mouth.
china: noun. Synonym of porcelain, a hard white translucent ceramic made from kaolin, now sometimes distinguished in reference to tableware as
chip: noun. A small piece broken from a larger piece of solid material.
dacha: noun. A Russian villa or summer house in the countryside.
dah: noun. The spoken representation of a dash in radio and telegraph Morse code.
daphnia: noun. A water flea of the genus Daphnia.
din: noun. A loud noise; a cacophony or loud commotion.
dip: noun. A lower section of a road or geological feature.
had: verb. simple past and past participle of have
hand: noun. The part of the forelimb below the forearm or wrist in a human, and the corresponding part in many other animals.
handicap: noun. Something that prevents, hampers, or hinders.
hap: noun. A person's lot (good or bad), luck, fortune, fate.
hic: interjection. An approximation to the sound of a hiccup, used e.g. to indicate drunkenness.
hin: noun. A former Hebrew liquid measure of volume (about 3.8 L).
hind: adjective. Located at the rear (most often said of animals' body parts).
hip: noun. The outward-projecting parts of the pelvis and top of the femur and the overlying tissue.
inch: noun. An English unit of length equal to 1/12 of a foot or 2.54 cm, conceived as roughly the width of a thumb.
naiad: noun. A female deity (nymph) associated with water, especially a spring, stream, or other fresh water.
nap: verb. To have a nap; to sleep for a short period of time, especially during the day.
nip: verb. To catch and enclose or compress tightly between two surfaces, or points which are brought together or closed; to pinch; to close in
nipa: noun. A palm tree of the species Nypa fruticans.
paca: noun. Any of the large rodents of the genus Cuniculus (but see also its synonyms), native to Central America and South America, which have
pad: noun. A flattened mass of anything soft, to sit or lie on.
padi: noun. Alternative form of paddy (“type of rice”).
pah: interjection. Used to express distaste, disgust or outrage.
paid: verb. simple past and past participle of pay
pain: noun. An ache or bodily suffering, or an instance of this; an unpleasant sensation, resulting from a derangement of functions, disease, or
pan: noun. A wide, flat receptacle used around the house, especially for cooking.
panda: noun. The red panda (Ailurus fulgens), a small raccoon -like animal of northeast Asia with reddish fur and a long, ringed tail.
panic: adjective. Alternative letter-case form of Panic (“pertaining to the Greek god Pan”).
phi: noun. Φ, the 21st letter of the Euclidean and modern Greek alphabet, usually romanized as "ph".
pia: noun. The pia mater, the innermost of the meninges that protect the brain and spinal cord.
pic: noun. A picture, especially a photographic image.
pica: noun. A disorder characterized by appetite and craving for non-edible substances, such as chalk, clay, dirt, ice, or sand.
pin: noun. A needle without an eye (usually) made of drawn-out steel wire with one end sharpened and the other flattened or rounded into a head,
pinch: verb. To squeeze a small amount of a person 's skin and flesh, making it hurt.
ach: noun. Any of several species of plants, such as smallage, wild celery, parsley.
adh: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Adhola.
apa: noun. A title used for male clergy or saints in the Coptic Church.
apaid: verb. simple past and past participle of apay
aph: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Athpare.
apinch: adverb. Pinching; so as to pinch.
chn: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Chinook Jargon.
cid: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Chimariko.
cpi: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Chinese Pidgin English.
dan: noun. A title of honour or respect similar to "master" or "Sir", used of historical and legendary figures of the past.
dana: noun. The virtue of generosity or giving, a form of alms, in Indian philosophy.
dha: noun. Alternative form of dah (“Burmese knife”).
dich: noun. Obsolete form of ditch (“a trench”).
hain: verb. To hedge or fence in; enclose; protect by hedging.
han: verb. plural simple present of have
hanap: noun. A rich goblet, especially one used on state occasions.
ida: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Idakho-Isukha-Tiriki.
inca: noun. Any of several species of hummingbirds in the genus Coeligena
napa: noun. Brassica rapa subsp. pekinensis, a kind of Chinese cabbage.
nid: noun. Alternative form of nide (“nest of pheasants”).
pada: noun. The basic metric unit of Vedic poetry, consisting of one line of verse in Sanskrit, typically as part of a four-line stanza.
paha: noun. An elongated landform composed of only loess, or of till capped by loess.
pahi: noun. A large war canoe of Tahiti.
pci: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Duruwa.
pdn: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Fedan.
phd: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Phudagi.
pid: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Piaroa.
pidan: noun. A traditional, hand-woven Cambodian silk textile used as a ceremonial hanging, canopy, or tapestry within Buddhist temples; often
pind: verb. To impound (as cattle), to shut up in a pound.
